relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Antioch holds a mixed prophetic and teaching community.
In worship and fasting, holy Spirit sets apart Barnabas and Saul.
Prayer, fasting, and hands release them into the work.
The mission begins as Spirit-sending.
The word is announced in Cyprus synagogues with John assisting.
At Paphos they meet Bar-Jesus, a false prophetic magician.
Sergius Paulus seeks to hear the word of the Divine.
Elymas opposes the trust and tries to twist the proconsul away.
Saul, now Paul, speaks filled with holy Spirit.
Twisted ways are named as adversarial opposition to justice.
Blindness falls for a season on the one twisting the straight path.
The proconsul trusts, astonished by the Lord’s teaching.
The mission sails on, while John returns to Jerusalem.
Paul and Barnabas enter Pisidian Antioch’s synagogue on Sabbath.
After Law and Prophets, a word of encouragement is invited.
Paul addresses Israel and Divine-reverers.
The story begins with chosen fathers and exodus power.
The wilderness years are remembered.
The land is given as inheritance.
Judges lead until Samuel.
Saul becomes king for forty years.
David is raised as the heart-aligned king.
From David’s seed comes the promised Savior/Healer, Jesus.
John prepares Israel through turning baptism.
John points beyond himself to the coming one.
The word of salvation/healing is sent to Abraham’s family and reverers.
Jerusalem’s rulers fulfill the prophets without recognizing the voices they read.
No death-cause is found, yet execution is requested.
Written things are completed, and he is laid in a tomb.
But the Divine raises him.
Those who walked with him become witnesses.
The ancestral promise becomes good news.
Resurrection fulfills sonship promise.
Raised life no longer returns to decay.
The Holy One will not see decay.
David served his generation and saw decay.
The raised one does not see decay.
Through this one, release of misalignments is announced.
Trust in him brings righteousness/justice beyond what Moses' Law could complete.
The prophets’ warning is brought near.
A divine work can be missed even when described.
The hearers ask for the word again next Sabbath.
Many follow and are urged to remain in grace.
Almost the whole city gathers for the word.
Jealousy contradicts the widening word.
Paul and Barnabas turn boldly toward the nations.
Light to the nations carries salvation/healing to earth’s end.
The nations rejoice and glorify the word.
The word spreads through the region.
Persecution is stirred through city influence.
Dust is shaken off as the mission moves to Iconium.
The disciples remain full of joy and holy Spirit.
